title: OneAgent installation behind proxy
failure_domains: Proxy configuration mismatch across hosts
Firewall or routing differences after network changes
TLS inspection, certificate trust, or proxy authentication issues
questions: What differs between working and failing hosts in proxy configuration or installer parameters?
What exact installer error appears on the failing hosts?
Can failing hosts resolve and reach the Dynatrace environment endpoint through the proxy?
Did the recent network change affect only specific subnets, VLANs, or security groups?
evidence: OneAgent installer logs from working and failing hosts
Output of env | grep -i proxy on affected hosts
curl -v or openssl connectivity test to Dynatrace endpoints through the proxy
Firewall, routing, and DNS comparison between working and failing hosts
mitigations: Set the proxy explicitly during installation and verify no conflicting proxy settings exist
Use a known-good host as a baseline and compare network and installer parameters
Temporarily bypass the proxy if policy allows to isolate the failure domain
escalate_when: Connectivity, proxy, and certificate checks are clean but the installer still fails reproducibly
The same failure occurs across multiple environments with identical validated configuration

title: OneAgent upgrade followed by missing host or process data
failure_domains: OneAgent service or module restart issue after upgrade
Version compatibility or rollback mismatch
Communication interruption between host and Dynatrace environment
questions: Did the issue begin immediately after the upgrade or after a later host change?
Is host monitoring affected, process monitoring affected, or both?
Were there any rollback attempts or partial upgrades on the impacted hosts?
evidence: Pre-upgrade and post-upgrade versions
OneAgent service status and relevant logs
Affected host list and scope of missing telemetry
mitigations: Verify agent service health and restart if appropriate
Compare the affected hosts against unaffected hosts on the same version
Rollback only after evidence is collected and change control allows it
escalate_when: Validated supported upgrade path still causes reproducible loss of telemetry
Multiple customers or environments show the same post-upgrade regression pattern

title: ActiveGate connectivity, routing, or capacity issue
failure_domains: Network path or proxy issue between ActiveGate and Dynatrace
Certificate or trust issue
ActiveGate sizing, queueing, or resource saturation
questions: Is the issue isolated to one ActiveGate or all ActiveGates in the network zone?
Have there been changes to certificates, proxies, or network zone routing?
Are resource utilization or queue metrics elevated on the impacted ActiveGate?
evidence: ActiveGate logs and version
Network zone and proxy configuration
CPU, memory, and queue or backlog indicators
mitigations: Verify outbound connectivity and certificate chain
Shift traffic or reduce load if one ActiveGate is saturated
Compare behavior across ActiveGates in the same topology
escalate_when: Validated network and resource conditions are healthy but failures persist
A reproducible regression appears tied to a version change

title: Logs missing after pipeline or processing change
failure_domains: Pipeline rules or processors are dropping or transforming records unexpectedly
Logs are ingested but no longer match dashboard or query assumptions
Retention, routing, or ingest path changed with the pipeline update
questions: Are the logs missing at ingest, missing after processing, or only missing in dashboards and queries?
What pipeline or processing rule changes were made before the issue began?
Can raw sample log lines still be found before transformations are applied?
Did field names, parsing logic, or retention rules change?
evidence: Sample raw log lines and expected output fields
Pipeline configuration before and after the change
Representative DQL or dashboard queries that stopped working
Timestamp range where the gap started
mitigations: Roll back or disable the recent pipeline change if operationally safe
Test raw ingest visibility separately from processed-query visibility
Validate whether dashboards depend on fields renamed by the pipeline
escalate_when: A validated supported pipeline configuration still drops data unexpectedly
The issue persists with a minimal reproducible pipeline configuration

title: Extension activation or runtime failure
failure_domains: Extension version compatibility or runtime requirement mismatch
Credential, permission, or endpoint connectivity problem
Activation pipeline failure after platform or extension upgrade
questions: Did the failure start after a platform upgrade, extension upgrade, or target system change?
Is the issue during activation, data collection, or only metric display?
Does the extension fail in all environments or only one?
evidence: Extension name, version, and activation logs
Target endpoint connectivity and authentication details
Platform version and upgrade timeline
mitigations: Validate extension compatibility and runtime prerequisites
Re-test with known-good credentials and endpoint connectivity
Roll back the extension version if supported and safe
escalate_when: Activation fails on supported versions with validated prerequisites
A reproducible regression appears only after a specific upgrade

title: Synthetic failures after frontend or application rollout
failure_domains: Synthetic monitor steps or selectors no longer match the updated application
The rollout changed network behavior or authentication flows used by the monitor
A monitor configuration or environment issue was exposed by the rollout
questions: Did the rollout change page structure, selectors, authentication, or API calls?
Are synthetic failures isolated to one location or all locations?
Does RUM still show successful real-user traffic for the same path?
evidence: Monitor execution details and screenshots
HAR or failing step traces
Deployment timeline and application changes
mitigations: Update monitor selectors or step logic if the application flow changed
Compare failing synthetic requests with real-user traffic
Temporarily reduce alert noise if the issue is clearly monitor-definition drift
escalate_when: A validated monitor fails on stable application behavior across locations
Platform-side monitor execution errors persist without app-side cause

title: RUM capture, tagging, or frontend instrumentation issue
failure_domains: RUM JavaScript tag changed or is no longer loaded correctly
CSP, ad blockers, or frontend bundling changes interfere with beacons
Application navigation model changed and instrumentation assumptions no longer hold
questions: Was the RUM snippet changed, relocated, or removed during deployment?
Are beacons blocked by CSP, browser policies, or frontend build changes?
Is the issue limited to one application version or browser segment?
evidence: Rendered page source showing the RUM tag
Browser network traces for beacon requests
CSP or frontend deployment changes
mitigations: Validate snippet placement and loading order
Check CSP and browser console/network errors
Compare affected and unaffected application versions
escalate_when: Validated snippet and network behavior still produce missing capture
A platform-side capture regression is reproducible across browsers

title: Missing Kubernetes metrics or events after cluster or operator change
failure_domains: Operator, Helm, or DaemonSet configuration drift after cluster change
RBAC or policy constraints block data collection in some namespaces
Cluster upgrade changed APIs, permissions, or webhook behavior
questions: Which namespaces are affected and what is common about them?
Did the operator, Helm chart, RBAC, or cluster policies change during the upgrade?
Are pod logs or admission events showing permission or injection failures?
evidence: Cluster and operator or Helm versions
Affected namespaces and pod-level symptoms
RBAC and policy changes around the time of the issue
mitigations: Validate operator and DaemonSet health
Compare affected namespaces with working namespaces for RBAC and policy differences
Reconcile deployment manifests if drift is detected
escalate_when: Supported cluster and operator combinations still fail reproducibly
The issue persists after validating RBAC, policies, and deployment health

title: API token, scope, or authentication failure
failure_domains: Token scopes or permissions no longer satisfy the workflow
Credentials rotated, expired, or are used against the wrong endpoint
Authentication flow or policy changed after administrative updates
questions: Which API endpoint and exact HTTP status code are failing?
Did token scopes, ownership, or permission policies change recently?
Is the issue limited to one token or all automation using the same auth path?
evidence: Endpoint URLs and HTTP status codes
Token type, scope set, and expiry details
Recent permission or policy changes
mitigations: Validate token scopes against the failing API calls
Re-test with a known-good token or service account
Confirm the automation is targeting the correct environment and endpoint
escalate_when: Supported auth flows fail with validated scopes and endpoints
The failure began after a platform change without customer-side auth changes

title: Missing custom or ingested metrics
failure_domains: Metrics are no longer sent or are rejected by the ingest path
Metric dimensions or names changed and dashboards no longer match
Quota, cardinality, or schema issues affect visibility
questions: Did the sender, schema, or dimensions change before the issue started?
Are metrics absent at ingest or only absent in charts and alerting?
Did volume or cardinality change significantly?
evidence: Sample metric payloads
Metric selector or query definitions
Timeline of schema or sender changes
mitigations: Validate payload schema and dimensions
Compare raw ingest success with dashboard selector behavior
Check whether dashboards rely on dimensions that changed
escalate_when: Supported metric ingestion fails with validated payloads
The platform rejects or hides valid metrics reproducibly

title: Unexpected Davis problem detection or alerting behavior
failure_domains: Configuration or threshold change altered alert behavior
Entity relationships or telemetry gaps changed Davis correlation outcomes
A recent rollout changed the symptom pattern presented to Davis
questions: Did thresholds, event settings, or problem notification rules change recently?
Is the issue false positives, missed alerts, or incorrect root-cause grouping?
Did telemetry quality or entity relationships change before the problem started?
evidence: Problem IDs and timestamps
Relevant alerting or event configuration
Examples of expected versus observed Davis behavior
mitigations: Review recent configuration changes and threshold tuning
Validate the underlying telemetry quality feeding Davis
Temporarily adjust noise-producing alerts if customer impact requires it
escalate_when: Documented configuration and telemetry are correct but correlation remains incorrect
A reproducible Davis regression appears after a version or platform change
